highlights.title: Oracle Data Center $16 Billion Financing Gets Over The Line
highlights.description: A $16 billion financing for a giant Oracle Corp. data center in Michigan has wrapped after months of stop-and-start negotiations with investors.

highlights.title: AI Chip Surge Elevates Taiwan, Korea in Global Equity Rankings
highlights.description: The artificial intelligence boom has triggered a seismic reshuffling of global equity markets, with Taiwan and South Korea muscling past European nations one by one.

highlights.title: Tesla offers 1 year of free Supercharging, claims ~40% premium for non-Tesla EVs
highlights.description:
Tesla is bringing back free Supercharging for new Model 3 Premium and Performance orders in North America, bundling one year of complimentary charging with new purchases.
The automaker also used the announcement to take a shot at non-Tesla EV owners using the Supercharger network, claiming they pay a “~40% premium” — but the actual figure is closer to 30-35% based on available data.
more…

highlights.title: China Says US Export Bills Risk Disrupting Chip Supply Chains
highlights.description: China said it is closely monitoring US legislative plans after the House Foreign Affairs Committee advanced export-control bills targeting semiconductors, which Beijing warned could disrupt global supply chains if passed.
